title: Switch to cycling for short commutes
description: Replace car journeys under 5km with cycling. This can save approximately 1.5 kg CO2 per trip for an average petrol car journey.
mao_motivation: Cycling reduces your carbon footprint significantly while improving cardiovascular health and saving fuel costs.
mao_ability: Start with shorter distances and gradually increase. Consider an e-bike if hills are challenging. Many cities now have dedicated cycle lanes.
mao_opportunity: Check local cycle-to-work schemes for tax-free bike purchases. Use route planners like Komoot to find safe cycling routes.

title: Use public transport instead of driving
description: Take the bus or train instead of driving alone. A bus journey produces about 50% less CO2 per km than a solo car trip.
mao_motivation: Public transport reduces per-person emissions by half or more compared to single-occupancy car trips.
mao_ability: Download local transport apps for real-time schedules. Many areas offer contactless payment on buses and trains.
mao_opportunity: Look into weekly/monthly travel passes which are often cheaper than fuel + parking costs.

title: Work from home one extra day per week
description: Eliminating one commute day per week reduces transport emissions by approximately 20%.
mao_motivation: Working from home saves commuting emissions, travel time, and costs while often improving work-life balance.
mao_ability: Set up a dedicated workspace at home. Use video conferencing for meetings that would otherwise require your presence.
mao_opportunity: Many employers now offer flexible working arrangements. Discuss options with your manager.

title: Carpool with colleagues
description: Sharing rides with 2-3 colleagues can cut your commute emissions by 50-66%.
mao_motivation: Carpooling halves or thirds your transport emissions while sharing fuel costs.
mao_ability: Coordinate schedules with nearby colleagues. Use carpooling apps to find matches.
mao_opportunity: Some workplaces offer priority parking for carpoolers. Check company travel plans.

title: Take trains instead of domestic flights
description: A train journey produces about 80% less CO2 than the equivalent domestic flight.
mao_motivation: Rail travel produces a fraction of aviation emissions. A London-Edinburgh train saves ~60 kg CO2 vs flying.
mao_ability: Book advance tickets for the best prices. Many routes are comparable in total journey time when including airport time.
mao_opportunity: Railcards offer 33% discounts. Book 6-12 weeks ahead for cheapest fares.

title: Switch to a renewable energy tariff
description: Choosing a 100% renewable electricity tariff can eliminate the carbon footprint of your home electricity use.
mao_motivation: Renewable energy tariffs can reduce your electricity carbon footprint to near zero, often at competitive prices.
mao_ability: Switching energy suppliers typically takes 5 minutes online. Your supply won't be interrupted.
mao_opportunity: Use comparison sites like Uswitch to find competitive renewable tariffs. Many are price-matched to standard tariffs.

title: Reduce thermostat by 1°C
description: Lowering your heating by 1°C can reduce your heating energy use by approximately 8-10%.
mao_motivation: A 1°C reduction is barely noticeable in comfort but saves both emissions and around £60-80/year on heating bills.
mao_ability: Simply turn your thermostat down by 1 degree. Wear an extra layer if needed.
mao_opportunity: Smart thermostats like Hive or Nest can optimise heating schedules automatically.

title: Install LED bulbs throughout your home
description: LED bulbs use 80% less energy than incandescent bulbs and last 25 times longer.
mao_motivation: LEDs save 80% energy on lighting. A typical home switching to all LEDs saves £30-40/year.
mao_ability: LED bulbs are available in all standard fittings and light temperatures. Simply swap them when old bulbs fail.
mao_opportunity: LED bulbs are now cheaper than ever, often under £2 each. They pay for themselves within months.

title: Use a smart power strip
description: Eliminate standby power consumption which can account for 5-10% of residential electricity use.
mao_motivation: Standby power waste is an easy win — smart strips automatically cut power to devices not in use.
mao_ability: Plug entertainment centres and computer setups into smart power strips. They automatically detect standby mode.
mao_opportunity: Available from £15-25 at most electronics retailers.

title: Wash clothes at 30°C instead of 40°C
description: Reducing wash temperature from 40°C to 30°C uses about 40% less energy per cycle.
mao_motivation: Modern detergents work effectively at 30°C. You save energy without compromising cleaning quality.
mao_ability: Simply select the 30°C programme on your washing machine. Use a quality detergent designed for low temperatures.
mao_opportunity: Most modern washing machines have eco programmes that optimise for low-temperature cleaning.

title: Have one meat-free day per week
description: Replacing meat with plant-based meals one day per week can save approximately 5-8 kg CO2 weekly.
mao_motivation: A single meat-free day weekly saves ~300 kg CO2 per year — equivalent to driving 1,500 km less.
mao_ability: Start with 'Meat-Free Mondays'. Explore vegetarian recipes from cuisines that are naturally plant-based (Indian, Mediterranean).
mao_opportunity: Supermarkets now offer extensive plant-based ranges. Many restaurants have dedicated vegetarian menus.

title: Replace beef with chicken or plant protein
description: Beef produces 4x more CO2 than chicken and 13x more than tofu per kg of protein.
mao_motivation: Switching from beef to chicken halves your meal emissions; switching to plant proteins reduces them by 80%+.
mao_ability: Try replacing beef mince with turkey/chicken mince or lentils in familiar recipes like bolognese and chilli.
mao_opportunity: Plant-based proteins are typically cheaper than beef. Many supermarkets now have dedicated plant-protein sections.

title: Reduce food waste by meal planning
description: The average UK household wastes £700 of food annually. Planning meals reduces waste and emissions from food production.
mao_motivation: Reducing food waste saves money and prevents the emissions from producing food that gets thrown away.
mao_ability: Plan meals for the week, write a shopping list, and check what you already have before shopping.
mao_opportunity: Use apps like Too Good To Go for discounted surplus food. Batch cooking saves time and reduces waste.

title: Choose seasonal and local produce
description: Seasonal, locally-grown food avoids the high emissions of air-freighted out-of-season produce.
mao_motivation: Air-freighted food can have 10-50x the transport emissions of locally grown alternatives.
mao_ability: Learn which fruits and vegetables are in season each month. Visit local farmers markets or farm shops.
mao_opportunity: Seasonal produce is often cheaper and fresher. Many areas have vegetable box delivery schemes.

title: Reduce dairy consumption
description: Dairy products, especially cheese, have significant carbon footprints. Plant-based alternatives can cut emissions substantially.
mao_motivation: Cheese produces 13.5 kg CO2/kg. Switching to plant milks saves about 60% of emissions vs cow's milk.
mao_ability: Try oat milk in coffee and cereal — it's the closest to dairy in taste. Gradually reduce cheese portions.
mao_opportunity: Plant milk varieties (oat, soya, almond) are widely available in all supermarkets and most cafes.

title: Buy secondhand clothing
description: Secondhand clothes produce 97% less CO2 than new garments, saving an average of 22 kg CO2 per item.
mao_motivation: Fast fashion is a major polluter. Buying secondhand gives clothes a second life and saves ~22 kg CO2 per garment.
mao_ability: Browse charity shops, Vinted, Depop, or eBay for quality secondhand clothing at fraction of retail prices.
mao_opportunity: Online platforms make secondhand shopping easy with search filters, size guides, and buyer protection.

title: Extend device lifespan by one year
description: Keeping your phone or laptop an extra year amortises manufacturing emissions over a longer period, reducing annual impact.
mao_motivation: 70-80% of a device's lifetime emissions come from manufacturing. Using it longer dramatically reduces annual carbon impact.
mao_ability: Use a case to protect your device. Replace batteries rather than the whole device. Keep software updated.
mao_opportunity: Right-to-repair legislation is improving availability of spare parts. Many local shops offer affordable repairs.

title: Use a reusable water bottle
description: Replacing single-use plastic bottles with a reusable bottle prevents plastic waste and production emissions.
mao_motivation: A reusable bottle pays back its carbon cost within 2-3 weeks of use. UK tap water is safe and virtually free.
mao_ability: Keep a reusable bottle in your bag. Refill at water fountains, cafes, or the Refill app's listed locations.
mao_opportunity: Many public spaces now have free water refill stations. The Refill app maps them across the UK.

title: Choose reusable bags for shopping
description: A reusable cotton bag used 50+ times has lower lifecycle emissions than single-use plastic bags.
mao_motivation: While individual bag emissions are small, the habit builds broader environmental awareness.
mao_ability: Keep reusable bags by the front door or in your car boot so you always have them available.
mao_opportunity: Many shops now charge for bags, making reusable bags the cheaper option.

title: Repair instead of replace
description: Repairing broken items instead of replacing them avoids the manufacturing emissions of new products.
mao_motivation: Repair culture reduces waste and manufacturing demand. A repaired appliance avoids 50+ kg CO2 from a new one.
mao_ability: Many repairs are simpler than expected. YouTube tutorials cover common fixes. Repair cafes offer free help.
mao_opportunity: Repair Cafes are free community events in many towns. iFixit provides free repair guides for electronics.

title: Air dry clothes instead of tumble drying
description: Tumble dryers are among the most energy-intensive household appliances. Air drying uses zero energy.
mao_motivation: A tumble dryer uses about 4.5 kWh per cycle. Air drying 3 loads/week saves ~700 kWh and £100+ per year.
mao_ability: Use a clothes airer indoors or a washing line outdoors. In winter, place near radiators for faster drying.
mao_opportunity: Foldable clothes airers are inexpensive and compact. Some areas have communal outdoor drying spaces.

title: Insulate your home properly
description: Good insulation can reduce heating energy demand by 25-40%, cutting both emissions and energy bills.
mao_motivation: Proper insulation is one of the highest-impact home improvements, saving £200-400/year on heating.
mao_ability: Start with draught-proofing (low cost, DIY). Loft insulation is the next most cost-effective measure.
mao_opportunity: Government grants (ECO scheme) may cover insulation costs for eligible households. Energy companies offer free assessments.

title: Take shorter showers
description: Reducing shower time by 2 minutes saves about 12 litres of hot water per shower.
mao_motivation: Heating water accounts for ~15% of home energy use. Shorter showers save water, energy, and money.
mao_ability: Use a shower timer or play a 4-minute song. Turn off water while lathering.
mao_opportunity: Water-saving showerheads maintain pressure while using 30-40% less water. Available from £10.

title: Choose digital over physical media
description: Digital books, music, and films avoid the manufacturing and shipping emissions of physical copies.
mao_motivation: Physical media involves raw materials, manufacturing, packaging, and shipping. Digital alternatives avoid most of these.
mao_ability: Use e-readers, streaming services, or library apps for books and media.
mao_opportunity: Libraries offer free digital lending through apps like Libby. Many streaming services bundle multiple media types.

title: Start composting food scraps
description: Composting diverts food waste from landfill, where it would produce methane — a potent greenhouse gas.
mao_motivation: Food waste in landfill produces methane, which is 28x more potent than CO2 as a greenhouse gas.
mao_ability: Start with a small kitchen caddy. Compost fruit/veg scraps, coffee grounds, and eggshells.
mao_opportunity: Many councils offer subsidised compost bins. Community gardens often welcome compost contributions.
